symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning, throbbing pain in the hand and forearm after a minor wrist sprain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Burning neuropathic-quality pain after a minor injury is characteristic of CRPS, though non-specific alone.
Severe hand pain occurs in infection too; not discriminating on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain disproportionate to the inciting minor injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continuing pain disproportionate to the inciting event is the mandatory cardinal feature of the Budapest CRPS criteria.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Allodynia is a sensory hallmark of CRPS reflecting central sensitisation.
Diffuse cutaneous allodynia to light brush is not a typical feature of septic arthritis, which produces joint-line pain on loading.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fluctuating dusky-blue/red skin discolouration with a cool hand (vasomotor instability)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vasomotor colour and temperature asymmetry is a Budapest-criteria vasomotor sign of CRPS.
A cool, colour-fluctuating hand is inconsistent with the fixed warm erythema of cellulitis.
Cool skin argues against a hot septic joint, though does not fully exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain dramatically worsened by ice/cold application

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cold intolerance and worsening with cold are typical of CRPS vasomotor dysfunction and have direct management implications (avoid ice).

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rigors, or systemic illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/systemic features lowers the probability of septic arthritis and cellulitis, though does not exclude early or localised infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regional, whole-hand and forearm involvement rather than a single joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regional distribution beyond a single nerve or joint territory is characteristic of CRPS.
Septic arthritis typically produces a single hot, swollen joint rather than diffuse regional involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-pitting oedema and temperature asymmetry of the hand on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Oedema and temperature asymmetry are Budapest-criteria sudomotor/oedema and vasomotor signs — clinician-observed, not available via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Livedo reticularis pattern on the dorsum of the hand on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Livedo reticularis reflects vasomotor dysregulation supporting CRPS; a clinician-observed examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inflammatory markers and white cell count (to help exclude infection)

loinc_ref.display: C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal CRP/ESR/WCC lower the probability of septic arthritis but do not fully exclude it; must be integrated with examination. Cannot be obtained via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome type I
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of disproportionate pain, allodynia, vasomotor colour/temperature change, cold intolerance and regional distribution after a minor inciting injury constitutes the clinical CRPS I (Budapest) pattern. No single feature is diagnostic; the gestalt, together with absence of infective features, points to CRPS while retaining the need to exclude infection in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical examination of the hand
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Examination for temperature asymmetry, oedema, joint-line tenderness/fluctuance and tendon integrity is the essential next step to support CRPS and exclude septic arthritis and tendon rupture. Cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Help exclude septic arthritis/deep-space infection when combined with examination. Normal results do not fully exclude infection but reduce probability.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration and synovial fluid analysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if examination raises suspicion of a single septic joint. Not a blanket recommendation; performed in person by the assessing clinician.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Doppler ultrasound of the upper limb for DVT
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Routine DVT imaging is not indicated once the allodynic, vasomotor CRPS pattern with regional distribution is recognised; reserve for cases where DVT suspicion genuinely persists on assessment. Ordering it reflexively over-investigates.
